summary: Nigeria has introduced the NOCOPO (Nigeria Open Contracting Portal) and the Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP) maintains an online portal for procurement information. However, adoption of e-procurement remains incomplete, with many agencies still relying on manual processes. The transition to fully digital procurement is ongoing.
notes: The Public Procurement Act 2007 established the BPP and introduced procurement reforms. NOCOPO was launched to improve transparency using open contracting principles. However, compliance with e-procurement requirements varies across federal ministries, departments, and agencies, and state-level procurement digitization is even more limited.
